## [03:01 - 06:06]

## [09:08 - 12:12]

I've also done this myself, but you get close to a prospect and you figure out what it is that they're into through conversation and things like that. You may find out that they're into golf. In this particular case, we had a prospect that we knew was into golf. We contacted someone in his office, figured out their shoe size, and then we sent them a pair of brand new Nike golf shoes because we knew that that was the type of shoe, that was the brand that they really liked. The secret was we only sent them one shoe. We sent them a note that said we were just trying to get a foot in the door. That they wanted the matching shoe for that pair that they had to take a meeting with us. Everybody talks about it, Dean talks about it, but sales is service. You want to think about what are the ways that you can actually be of service to your customers? Is it returning a favor and giving them a referral in advance? Maybe it's actually offering a free coaching session or a free module to your course or the entire course itself based on them giving you a referral. The last thing is you want to be courageous. But if you don't make the ask, you're highly unlikely to actually get the referral. Be memorable, be of service, and be courageous. Let's talk about the don'ts. Don't be shy. When you ask for a referral, you want to make sure that you ask and you do it in a way that lets people know that you're on a mission to create an impact and that this is an opportunity for people to join you in creating that impact by offering a referral and helping someone else to get in your world and get the results or the outcome that you promised to deliver. ## [12:12 - 15:04]

Brandy referenced your warm network in a previous video, but your network really makes up everybody that you're connected with.
